source: 0. Neural Machine Translation of Rare Words with Subword Units.pdf

concepts.finding: Byte Pair Encoding (BPE) segments words into subword units based on frequency, enabling NMT to handle rare and unseen words as sequences of subword units.
concepts.implication: Subword tokenization solves the open-vocabulary problem and is directly applicable to query understanding: rare queries and misspellings can be handled via subword units without OOV issues.
concepts.open_question: What is the optimal subword vocabulary size for search queries given their short length and high variability?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: BPE-based tokenization with a 16K subword vocabulary will reduce OOV rate in search queries to <0.1% while maintaining semantic fidelity for intent classification.

concepts.finding: BPE-based NMT achieves BLEU scores comparable to or exceeding word-based models, demonstrating subword units preserve translation quality while eliminating unknown words.
concepts.implication: Subword representations are not just practical but maintain full task quality, making them suitable for high-stakes search applications.
concepts.open_question: Does subword vs. word-level tokenization affect query intent classification differently for short vs. long queries?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Subword tokenization improves intent classification F1 for short queries (<3 words) by 5%+ compared to word-level tokenization.
